commentary: Babe, ten activities versus eleven is basically a rounding error, and what you did with those ten was tidier and more intentional than anything from last week's scattered Friday triple-dip situation. Four pool sessions at Prince Alfred with heart rates sitting consistently in the 140s shows you are working those laps, not floating through them, and Monday's opening swim at 132 before weights is the kind of disciplined warm-up that a man who knows what he's doing walks in with. The Mosman circuit came in at 13.3 kilometres against last Saturday's 13.5, which is close enough that nobody's crying, but the heart rate crept up to 155 from 149, so the legs were earning it a little harder this time. That Tuesday so-called recovery run at 150 average heart rate is absolutely sending me, because honey, that is not recovery, that is just running with a cute name on it, and I respect the audacity completely.
metrics_commentary: Twenty and a half kilometres across two sessions and nearly nine kilometres in the pool — baby, that is a body that showed up this week, hard. The HRV is the real headline though: 62 ms against a seven-day average of 51 ms means your nervous system is not just recovering, it is thriving, and that gap is basically your body winking at you. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.7 tells the same story — that is a genuinely athletic number, the kind that makes a certain type of outdoorsy guy on Grindr very interested in your cardio. The only thing worth watching is whether those two run sessions are enough to keep the legs honest alongside all that pool work, because the swim volume is doing the heavy lifting right now and the running deserves a little more attention next week.
gym_commentary: Three sessions out of four is a miss on paper, but those arms told a different story this week — barbell curls up to a 35.0 kg e1RM from 31.5 kg, and dumbbell bench hitting 34.8 kg from 31.7 kg, both in the same week, which is the kind of double PR that makes a man look at himself differently in the mirror. Volume dropped to 20,148 kg from 24,518 kg, which is the honest consequence of skipping that fourth session, and Scott will have noticed. Still, the strength trend is pointing exactly where it should — up and getting louder about it — and a body pressing 76.0 kg on incline barbell, pulling 84.0 kg on lat pulldown, hinging 107.9 kg on the trap bar, and leg pressing 168.0 kg is not a body that is quietly becoming anything. Get the fourth session back next week, baby.
